zh.French Flanders, France: 法國福藍德 福藍薩
zh.Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France: 法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德利勒

zh.lifeSummary: 當Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE於1220年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德利勒時，他的父親Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin33歲，母親Marotte de La Wez30歲。他於1259年與Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦福林結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1288年5月在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德巴姆享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE于1220年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德利勒时，他的父亲Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin33岁，母亲Marotte de La Wez30岁。他于1259年与Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre在法国豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德瓦福林结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1288年5月在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德巴姆享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: LE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: BORGNE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bjorn, Jan Erik.
Danish and Norwegian: variant of Borg , the definite form singular of borg ‘fortification, stronghold’.
Norwegian: in some cases, a compound of two elements, borg ‘fortification, stronghold’ and Old Norse vin ‘meadow’, e.g. the name written as Byrghini in 1331, in the province of Akershus.

variants.name: Borg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Swen, Bjorn, Evald, Jorgen, Karsten, Knut, Lennart, Nels.

Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from various farms and other minor places so named, from Old Norse borg ‘fortification, stronghold’. In some cases the name is topographic, often referring to a hill that resembles a fortification, rather than an actual fortification.

Swedish: ornamental name from borg ‘fortified castle’ (compare 1 above), or a habitational name from a place called Borg or from a placename containing the word borg. This surname is also found in Finland.

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: LANGLEE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Langley or Longley (in Kent, Sussex, Hertfordshire, Norfolk, Somerset, Shrops, Lancashire, Durham, and many other counties), themselves from Old English lang ‘long’ + lēah ‘wood, glade, clearing’.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Langlif (Old Norse Langlíf ‘long’ + ‘life’ or ‘body’).
Americanized form of French   Langlais  .

variants.name: Langlais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Marcel, Normand, Albertine, Armand, Cecile, Enoil, Herve, Jacques, Laurent.
French: ethnic name for an Englishman, from Anglais ‘Englishman’, with fused definite article l'. Compare Langley .

History: Daniel Sargent dit Sérien and Langlais from Worcester, MA married Marguerite Lavoie in Rivière-Ouelle, QC, in 1718. Joseph Weber dit Langlais from Boston, MA married Marie-Charlotte Guillet in Cap Saint-Ignace, QC, in 1725.

variants.name: Longley
meanings: English (mainly Nottinghamshire and South Yorkshire): variant of   Langley  . Compare   Longpre   2.

nameMeanings.name: Baudouin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Baldwin .

en.lifeSummary: When Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE was born in 1220, in Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, his father, Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin, was 33 and his mother, Marotte de La Wez, was 30. He married Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre in 1259, in Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in May 1288, in Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE est né en 1220, à Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Marotte de La Wez, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre en 1259, à Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en mai 1288, à Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE 1220 in Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin 33 und seine Mutter Marotte de La Wez 30. Er hat 1259 in Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb im Mai 1288 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE è nato il nel 1220 nel Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, il suo padre, Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Marotte de La Wez, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre nel 1259, nel Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel maggio 1288, nel Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEEが1220年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードリルで生まれた時，父Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrinは33歳，母Marotte de La Wezは30歳であった。1259年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードワvリンでMarie Jacquemine e La Bourreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1288年5月にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードロンメで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE(이)가 1220년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 릴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Marotte de La Wez(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1259년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 와브린에서 Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1288년 5월 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 롬에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE nasceu em 1220, em Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Marotte de La Wez, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre em 1259, em Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em maio de 1288, em Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE родился в 1220 г. в местности лилл, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Marotte de La Wez, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre в 1259 г. в местности ваврин, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер май 1288 г. в местности ломм, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baudouin LE BORGNE DE LANGLEE nació en 1220, en Lille, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Baudouin II Robert de Wavrin Chevalier, Seigneur, de Wavrin, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Marotte de La Wez, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Marie Jacquemine e La Bourre en 1259, en Wavrin, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en mayo de 1288, en Lomme, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, a la edad de 68 años.
